# Foam-Agent run history: the run ledger

The MCP server records every simulation in `runs/ledger.md` as a mechanical
side effect of the run lifecycle — `resolve_case_dir` inserts a `planned`
row, `run_case` (and the SLURM submit/status pair) flips it through
`running → done/debugging` and stamps the physics verdict. Nobody does
bookkeeping: not you, not the user. This skill is about reading that record
well and using the one sanctioned write.

Two rules govern everything below:

- **Read cheap.** `runs/ledger.md` answers most questions by itself. Open a
  case's logs only when the user asks for detail the ledger cannot answer.
- **Write only through `set_run_note`.** Never edit `ledger.md` with a file
  tool — hand-editing the ledger is off-limits for you. Every cell except
  Notes is machine-owned, and the Notes column is reserved for the HUMAN's
  hand edits; your note-writing goes through the tool too.

## 1. Read the ledger first

`runs/ledger.md` is one markdown table, newest row first, with columns
`ID | Case | Created | Solver | Mesh | Status | Result | Key result | Notes`.

- **Status is pipeline state, Result is the physics verdict** — separate
  facts. `done` + `diverged` is a run that finished and blew up; never
  present it as a good result. The complete vocabulary (the only legal
  values) is in `runs/run-states.yml` beside the ledger.
- The runs directory lives in the clone (bind-mounted into the Docker
  container), so your local file tools read the same `ledger.md` the server
  writes. Can't see the repo's `runs/`? `read_case_file` with the runs
  directory as `case_dir` and `ledger.md` as the path reads it through the
  server.
- Hide `archived` rows by default; include them only when the user asks
  ("all runs", "including archived ones").

Typical questions, all answerable without opening a single log file:

- **"List my runs"** → present the non-archived rows (ID, Case, Status,
  Result, Notes — condense to what fits the conversation).
- **"What happened to the dam break run?"** → find the row by Case
  (substring match: "dam break" ≈ `dam_break`), answer from Status +
  Result + Key result + Notes.
- **"Which runs failed?"** → rows with Result `diverged`, plus Status
  `debugging` for runs whose rescue is still in flight.

## 2. Zoom into logs only on demand

When the user wants more than the row holds ("WHY did it diverge?", "what
was the final residual?"), that is the cue to open the case's own artifacts:
`runs/<case>/log.<solver>` (the tail — errors and final residuals are at the
end), `Allrun.out` / `Allrun.err`, or `postProcessing/`. Give the ledger's
answer first, then dig.

## 3. Notes and archiving — through `set_run_note` only

`set_run_note` is the ONLY sanctioned skill-side ledger write. Arguments:

- `id` — from the ID column (zero-padded like `0003`; plain `3` is accepted)
- `note` — REPLACES the Notes cell (`''` clears it; omit to leave it alone).
  Because it replaces: when the user means to ADD to an existing note, read
  the current cell first and include it in the new text.
- `archive` — `true` archives, `false` unarchives, omit to leave Status
  alone.

Facts to respect:

- Archiving a run whose Result is still `pending` stamps it `abandoned`
  ("owner stopped pursuing this") — tell the user when that happens.
- Unarchiving restores Status to `done` and leaves Result as it is —
  `abandoned` stays until a new run overwrites it through the lifecycle.
  Only archived (or already-done) rows can be unarchived; anything else
  fails with the ledger untouched — unarchiving is not a way to set Status.
- Unknown ID → the tool errors and the ledger is untouched. Re-read the
  ledger and retry with the right ID.
- "Archive the old cylinder runs" → one `set_run_note(id, archive=true)`
  call per matching row; confirm the list with the user first when "old"
  is ambiguous.

## 4. Comparing runs

"Compare Cd across the cylinder runs" → collect the matching rows and put
their Key result, Result and Solver columns side by side — that is the
ledger-level answer. Be honest about fidelity: stamping is deliberately
coarse for now (exit status plus a blow-up scan feed the Result verdict,
and Key result is typically still `-`); the solver-log convergence parser
(the next spec) will improve it. When Key result is empty and the user
wants real numbers, offer the log dive: extract the quantity from each
case's logs or `postProcessing/` — an on-demand read, invited by the user.

## 5. The zero-token alternative (mention when relevant)

`python scripts/runs.py` pretty-prints the same ledger deterministically,
no agent and no tokens: `--all` includes archived rows, `--status VALUE`
and `--result VALUE` filter. Suggest it when the user wants a quick check
that costs nothing. The ledger is also plain markdown — opening
`runs/ledger.md` in any editor is always legitimate for the human.

## 6. When the ledger looks wrong

A row whose case directory is gone? A case directory with no row (e.g. a
runs directory that predates the ledger)? Don't fix it by hand — run the
deterministic repair:

```
python scripts/ledger_check.py          # dry-run report (the default)
python scripts/ledger_check.py --fix    # repair, after a timestamped backup
```

It adopts orphan case directories as new rows (also the migration path for
pre-ledger runs directories), archives rows whose directories vanished
(never deletes), renumbers duplicate IDs, and repairs illegal Status/Result
values. Run `--fix` while the server is idle — its lockfile guards against
concurrent repairs, not against the server's own writes.
